summary: The data industry faces a fundamental choice between vertically-integrated mega platforms controlled by big clouds and a horizontally-integrated ecosystem of interoperable open technologies. While horizontal integration is slower and requires extensive coordination (the 'tortoise' approach), it offers better long-term maintainability and staying power. AI complicates this picture because model training costs currently favor plugging into major vendors' APIs.
key_points: Tension between vertical integration (big clouds, out-of-box solutions) and horizontal integration (open, composable, best-of-breed)
Horizontal integration requires 'open source diplomacy' and coordination but enables incremental improvement without tight coupling
Open source eventually won in software development; data is catching up
AI creates new challenges for horizontal integration due to expensive model training costs
The ultimate question is whether AI/data infrastructure will be owned by megacorporations or remain open
money_quotes: It's like the tortoise and the hare approach. So I think the modular data stack, the horizontal approach, is definitely the tortoise approach. It requires a lot of open source diplomacy, a lot of coordination across projects, getting people to agree to specialize and then work together toward productive ends.

What world do you want to live in? A vertically integrated one or one where it's horizontally integrated and best of breed? But it's really challenging to deliver cohesive user experiences in the horizontal integration world.
I'd hate to see a world where the AI-Industrial Complex is the gatekeeper to models. It already sucks seeing the big clouds as the tollroads for many companies' tech and data workloads.
The big question is who owns this stack - a handful of big megacorporations or nobody? For innovation and humanity's sake, I very much hope it's the latter.
key_insight: The data industry's future hinges on whether the slow, collaborative work of building interoperable open technologies can provide a viable alternative to vertically-integrated platforms before AI consolidates power in a handful of megacorporations.
